strengths: Strong SQL and complex query experience
Quantified impact on KPIs and efficiency
weaknesses: Limited tenure in senior data analyst roles (experience ~3 years)
No explicit advertising/ad sales domain experience
No explicit mention of data warehouse architecture beyond tools
improvement_bullets: Optimized data warehouse structures and schemas within PostgreSQL and cloud environments, ensuring high-performance data modeling to support complex ad sales reporting requirements.

Collaborated with product teams to define business requirements for BI platforms, demonstrating a high aptitude for mastering new reporting software such as Microstrategy or similar enterprise-grade tools.
Delivered compelling presentations of data-driven insights to executive stakeholders, utilizing data storytelling to translate complex technical findings into actionable business strategies.
Managed full-cycle campaign measurement and performance reporting, tracking ad-driven KPIs to provide deep-dive analysis on audience engagement and revenue growth.
Engineered scalable data models and warehouse architectures that integrated disparate sources, facilitating reliable reporting for multi-platform media products.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst opportunity with Disney. I am interested in this opportunity because of Disney’s unique position at the intersection of world-class storytelling and advanced media technology. What interests me about working for Disney is its commitment to scaling digital advertising products that reach millions of global users, which inspires me because I am passionate about using data to enhance the consumer experience. I believe my expertise in building scalable data pipelines and my ability to derive actionable insights from complex datasets would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the 'why' behind the metrics. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified that manual reporting was causing significant delays in decision-making. I built an automated end-to-end data pipeline using Python and Power BI to replace legacy processes. This shift reduced data processing time by 40%, allowing leadership to reallocate resources toward customer acquisition, which contributed to a 300% increase in new customers over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst role at Disney would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and cloud-based modeling to drive Disney’s advertising product performance. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my cross-functional documentation and systems-thinking skills. I collaborated with Product and Underwriting teams to optimize relational database queries and clean insurance testing data, which improved data accuracy by 30% and significantly reduced project delivery delays. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and automated reporting to support the Ad Platforms team in defining the future of Disney’s digital business. I know that my analytical rigor and cross-functional experience align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
